vehicles.slot: Lift rubber mat in storage compartment BEHIND center console — two access holes
shift: Lift the rubber mat in the storage tray BEHIND the center console shifter.
Two holes are exposed. Insert a screwdriver into the correct hole.
Pull the shifter trigger while pushing the selector toward Neutral.
Alternatively: remove the air cleaner box under hood and disconnect the cable from the transmission shift lever — two clicks equals Neutral.
Confirm N before moving vehicle.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under hood.
Connect jump pack. Restore power.
Use rubber mat / access hole method above.
AWD: flatbed strongly recommended.
vehicles.notes: Slot is BEHIND the shifter, under rubber mat in storage tray — not at the shifter base.
vehicles.warn: AWD Haldex angle gear — flatbed strongly preferred. Do not wheel-lift without flatbed.
vehicles.towMethod: AWD: FLATBED STRONGLY PREFERRED. If wheel-lift required: rear lift only, short distance, slow speed. Haldex damage risk is high with prolonged wheel-lift.
towNotes: Haldex AWD angle gear is easily damaged by improper towing. Flatbed always preferred.
If wheel-lift must be used: rear lift, under 35 mph, under 35 miles.
Parking brake electronic issues reported — confirm brake functionality.

knownIssues.title: Shift Release Is BEHIND the Shifter, Not at Base
knownIssues.detail: The override access holes are under the rubber mat in the storage compartment BEHIND the center console, not at the shifter base. Many operators probe the base area and cannot find it. Lift the mat in the tray behind the shifter.

vehicles.slot: Console storage tray BEHIND shifter — two holes under rubber mat (same as 2003-2014)
shift: Lift rubber mat in storage tray BEHIND shifter.
Insert screwdriver into override hole.
Pull the shifter tab while moving selector to Neutral.
Confirm N on display.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
12V battery under hood. 48V mild-hybrid: DO NOT touch 48V battery.
Connect jump pack to 12V under-hood terminals ONLY.
Restore power, then use override procedure above.
T8 PHEV: 12V only — never touch orange HV cables or HV battery.
vehicles.notes: 48V mild-hybrid on some trims. T8 PHEV: orange HV system present.
vehicles.warn: 48V: NEVER use 48V battery to jump — lethal voltage. T8 PHEV: HV system present.
vehicles.towMethod: AWD: FLATBED ONLY for T8 PHEV and Recharge. Standard AWD: flatbed strongly preferred.
towNotes: 48V MILD-HYBRID: Never jump from the 48V support battery. 12V only.
T8 PHEV: FLATBED ONLY. Orange cables indicate HV system — do not touch.
AWD angle gear damage risk with wheel-lift — flatbed always preferred.

epb.override: Connect 12V to restore power, then release EPB normally via button. Manual: T40 Torx to manually retract caliper piston at rear calipers.
steps: PREFERRED: Connect jump pack to 12V under-hood terminals.
Once power restored, press and hold EPB button to release.
MANUAL (no power available): Locate T40 Torx socket.
At each rear caliper, use T40 Torx to manually retract the EPB actuator piston.
This requires removing the wheel for caliper access.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock. Requires ignition power.

battery.location: Under hood (12V). 48V support battery separate — never jump from 48V.
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — 12V terminals only. NEVER use 48V system.
battery.warnings: 48V mild-hybrid: lethal voltage — never jump from 48V. T8 PHEV: 12V auxiliary only, never touch HV.

knownIssues.title: 48V Mild-Hybrid Jump Hazard
knownIssues.detail: SPA-platform XC90s with mild-hybrid have a 48V support battery. Under no circumstances should this battery be used for jump starting. The 48V system can cause lethal shock. Always use the 12V under-hood terminals only.

vehicles.trans: 6-speed automatic or 8-speed automatic (2018+); T8 PHEV available
vehicles.slot: Center console near shifter base — cap or access hole under rubber mat
shift: Locate cap or access point near selector base or under rubber mat behind shifter.
Remove cap / lift mat.
Insert key, press plunger while pressing brake.
Move selector to N.
Confirm N on display.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is in TRUNK under cargo floor panel (many models).
Use engine compartment charging terminals (marked + with cover) for jumping.
Negative at right engine mount outer screw or grounding bolt.
Restore power, shift normally or use override.
T8 PHEV: 12V only — never touch HV system.
vehicles.notes: Battery often in trunk — ALWAYS use engine compartment jump terminals, not trunk battery directly.
vehicles.warn: AWD: flatbed preferred. Battery in trunk — use engine compartment terminals.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear (front drive wheels roll). AWD: flatbed preferred. T8 PHEV AWD: FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: Battery under cargo floor — never jump directly at trunk battery. Use engine compartment posts.
AWD Haldex: flatbed preferred to avoid angle gear damage.
T8 PHEV: flatbed only — regenerative braking and HV system.

epb.override: Connect 12V to engine compartment terminals to restore power, then release EPB via button. Manual release requires T40 Torx at rear calipers.
steps: Connect jump pack to engine compartment charging terminals (+ marked, − at engine mount).
Press and hold EPB button to release.
Manual: T40 Torx to retract rear caliper pistons if no power available.

battery.location: TRUNK under cargo floor panel (most models). Engine compartment has jump terminals.
battery.jumpTerminals: Engine compartment: positive under plastic cover marked '+'; negative at right engine mount outer screw
battery.warnings: NEVER jump at trunk battery directly — use engine compartment terminals. T8 PHEV: 12V only.

knownIssues.title: Battery Is in Trunk — Use Engine Compartment Terminals
knownIssues.detail: The main 12V battery on most XC60 models is under the cargo floor in the trunk. Do NOT attempt to jump directly at the trunk battery. Volvo provides dedicated jump-start terminals in the engine compartment (positive post with cover, negative at engine mount bolt). Always use these terminals.

vehicles.drivetrain: FWD or AWD; Recharge: AWD electric
vehicles.slot: Electronic selector with mechanical override near console base
shift: Locate mechanical override near selector base.
Insert key or flat tool, press plunger.
Press brake. Move selector to N.
Recharge EV: use touchscreen Service menu or restore 12V for touchscreen access.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery in engine compartment (gas) or frunk/under hood (Recharge).
Connect 12V jump pack to engine compartment terminals.
Restore power. Shift normally or use override.
Recharge: 12V auxiliary only — NEVER touch HV battery.
vehicles.notes: Recharge (EV): FLATBED ONLY. 12V auxiliary under hood.
vehicles.warn: Recharge EV: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: Gas AWD: flatbed preferred. Gas FWD: rear lift. Recharge EV: FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: Recharge EV: flatbed only — electric motors damaged by rolling on ground.
Gas AWD: Haldex system — flatbed preferred.
Use engine compartment jump terminals on all models.

epb.override: Connect 12V to restore power, then EPB button release. Manual T40 Torx at rear calipers.
steps: Connect jump pack to engine compartment 12V terminals.
Restore power. Press and hold EPB button to release.
If no power possible: T40 Torx at rear calipers to manually retract pistons.

battery.warnings: Recharge EV: flatbed only. 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV.

vehicles.slot: Center console — access point near shifter base or under rubber mat behind shifter
shift: Locate override slot at shifter base or lift rubber mat in tray behind shifter.
Insert key, press plunger.
Press brake. Move to N.
Some models: press brake pedal first, then use override, then move shifter.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery in TRUNK under floor panel (primary). Stop-start models may have second battery.
Use engine compartment jump terminals — positive under small black plastic cover marked '+'; negative at fuse box area.
Restore power. Shift normally or use slot.
T8 PHEV: 12V only.
vehicles.notes: Stop-start equipped models may have dual 12V batteries. Battery in trunk — use engine compartment terminals.
vehicles.warn: Battery in trunk — always use engine compartment jump terminals. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear (front drive wheels roll). AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: Battery in trunk — never jump at trunk battery directly.
Dual battery on stop-start models — engine compartment terminals handle both.

epb.override: Restore 12V via engine compartment terminals, then EPB button. Manual T40 Torx at rear calipers.
steps: Connect jump pack to engine compartment terminals (+/−).
Press and hold EPB button to release.
Manual fallback: T40 Torx at rear EPB caliper actuators.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock on newer push-button start models.

battery.location: TRUNK under floor panel. Engine compartment has jump terminals.
battery.jumpTerminals: Engine compartment: small cover marked '+'; negative at fuse box area
battery.warnings: Always use engine compartment terminals — not trunk battery directly. T8 PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Modern electronic shifter — mechanical emergency release under console
shift: Press brake pedal.
Use electronic shifter to select N.
If no power: locate mechanical override in center console (under console trim near shifter base).
Insert key, engage override plunger, press brake, move to N.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery in TRUNK under floor (primary).
Connect jump pack at engine compartment terminals (positive marked '+', negative at mount).
Restore power. Shift normally.
T8 PHEV: 12V only — never touch HV system.
vehicles.notes: Electronic systems require power for all functions. Battery in trunk — engine compartment terminals mandatory.
vehicles.warn: AWD: flatbed preferred. Battery in trunk — use engine compartment terminals.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear. AWD: flatbed preferred. T8 AWD: FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: Fully electronic steering and shifting — requires power for safe operation.

epb.override: Restore 12V via engine compartment terminals, EPB button release. Manual: T40 Torx at rear calipers.
steps: Connect jump pack to engine compartment 12V terminals.
Restore power. Hold EPB button to release.
Manual fallback: T40 Torx at rear caliper EPB actuators.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock — requires ignition power.

battery.location: TRUNK under floor panel (primary). Engine compartment has jump terminals.
battery.jumpTerminals: Engine compartment — positive under cover marked '+'; negative at engine mount
battery.warnings: Always use engine compartment terminals. T8 PHEV: 12V only.
